evals.prompt: I need to pull CAD IG OAS spreads (the broad Canadian investment grade index) from 2018 to today, daily. I also want to know where spreads are right now vs history — tight, wide, how many standard deviations out, that kind of thing. Generate me the Python code to do it.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that fetches cad_oas (I05510CA Index, INDEX_OAS_TSY_BP) from 2018, and also calls context() or equivalent to show percentile, Z-score, and regime label for the current spread level.
expectations: The code fetches I05510CA Index with INDEX_OAS_TSY_BP field (or uses cad_oas alias)
The code generates a spread intelligence summary including percentile rank vs history
The code includes a Z-score or standard deviation calculation for current spreads
The code uses the correct start date of 2018 or earlier for history context
The code uses uv run python or references the virtual environment for execution

evals.prompt: Pull the US IG and US HY excess return indices from January 2015 to today, on a weekly frequency. I want cumulative index values (base 100), not YTD percentages. Save the result to a CSV. Write me the Python code.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that fetches LUACTRUU Index and LF98TRUU Index with INDEX_EXCESS_RETURN_YTD, converts to chain-linked cumulative indices (base 100), uses weekly periodicity, and saves to CSV.
expectations: The code fetches LUACTRUU Index and LF98TRUU Index with INDEX_EXCESS_RETURN_YTD field
The code converts YTD percent returns to cumulative chain-linked indices (not raw YTD values)
The code sets weekly periodicity (W or WEEKLY)
The code uses January 2015 as the start date
The code saves the output to a CSV file with UTF-8 encoding

evals.prompt: I need to compare all the CAD IG sector spreads: fins, non-fins, utilities, A-rated, BBB-rated, plus the provincial spreads. Give me all of them in a single DataFrame, daily, from 2010 onwards. Write the Python code.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that fetches at least 6 CAD IG sector tickers (I05523CA, I05520CA, I05517CA, I05515CA, I05516CA, I34069CA) all with INDEX_OAS_TSY_BP, merges them into one DataFrame.
expectations: The code fetches at least 5 of the 6 CAD sector tickers (fins, non-fins, uts, A, BBB, provs)
All sector tickers use INDEX_OAS_TSY_BP as the Bloomberg field
The result is a single merged DataFrame (not separate Series objects)
The code uses 2010 or earlier as the start date
The sector columns are correctly named or labeled (not just numeric indices)
